title: Euchiton limosus
intro: Euchiton limosus, also known as the Woolly Pustulberry, is a small, shrub-like plant native to Australia and New Zealand. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat a range of health issues, including sore throats, coughs, and skin irritations, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Euchiton limosus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, which are often used to soothe sore throats, calm skin irritations, and support immune function.
